items.headline: BofA Securities Adjusts Price Target on Albemarle to $155 From $225, Maintains Buy Rating
items.snippet: Albemarle (ALB) has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $207.56, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

items.headline: RBC Cuts Price Target on Albemarle to $166 From $257, Keeps Outperform Rating
items.snippet: Albemarle (ALB) has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $207.56, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

items.headline: Will ALB's Expansion Actions Power Stronger Sales Volumes?

items.headline: Albemarle Stock Loses 25% in a Month: What Should Investors Do Now?
items.snippet: Albemarle Corporation's ALB shares have tumbled 25.5% in the past month. Falling lithium market prices are weighing on the ALB stock lately, amid slowing EV demand in China, an inventory glut and prospects of increased supply from mine restarts (CATL Jianxiawo, Mineral Resources

items.headline: The U.S. Is Increasing Efforts to Rebuild Its Rare Earth Supply Chain
items.snippet: Albemarle is the world's largest lithium producer. Q1 2026 adjusted EBITDA jumped 148% YoY to $664 million as lithium prices rebounded from 2025 lows; the company retired $1.3 billion of debt during the quarter.

items.headline: Lithium Is on the Rise Again. Does That Make Albemarle a Buy?
items.snippet: Analysts' average price target is $214.65. The stock trades for less than 12 times forward earnings, a discount to intrinsic value.
